Rhaponticum strictum is a plant species in the sunflower family, native to Central Asia, with a long history of traditional use in folk medicine.

The health benefits of Rhaponticum strictum include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and adaptogenic properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being.

The bioactive constituents of Rhaponticum strictum include sesquiterpene glycosides, such as 11, 13-dihydroxy-3, 14-dihydro-9-keto-gamma-sesquiphellandrene, and other triterpenoids and flavonoids.
